$comment: Single source of truth for the EQ 5-tier role model + permission matrix. Extracted from eq-shell src/session.ts + src/permissions/**. Edit this file and run `npm run build` to regenerate roles.ts / roles.json. Adding a tier, permission, or roleAlias is a spec-level change — keep IDENTITY-MODEL.md in sync.

roles.label: Manager
roles.description: Full admin — user management, audit view + rollback, every module action.

roles.label: Supervisor
roles.description: Audit view, intake commit, equipment edit. No user management.

roles.label: Employee
roles.description: Intake view + import, equipment view. No commit, no admin.

roles.label: Labour Hire
roles.description: Minimal — own roster / timesheet only (no shell permissions today).

platformAdmin.effect: Orthogonal to role. When true, can() short-circuits to true for every permission across every tenant. EQ-internal staff only (single audit point for cross-tenant power).

$labels: label = short plain-English name for admin UIs (what a non-technical manager reads when granting access). description = the longer developer-facing explanation. Keep labels verb-first and jargon-free per EQ voice.

permissions.label: Invite users
permissions.description: Invite a new user to the tenant.

permissions.label: Manage access groups
permissions.description: Create and manage security groups + membership.

permissions.label: View records
permissions.description: View canonical records (customers, sites, contacts, assets).

permissions.label: Delete records
permissions.description: Delete a canonical record (hard delete, manager-only).

permissions.label: Start an import
permissions.description: Initiate an import (drop file, map, validate).

permissions.label: Confirm an import
permissions.description: Commit a validated batch to canonical (destructive).

permissions.label: View equipment
permissions.description: See the Plant & Equipment list + calibration.

permissions.label: Submit onboarding
permissions.description: Submit or manage an onboarding card.

permissions.label: View maintenance
permissions.description: View the Service CMMS (work orders, defects, PPM).

permissions.label: Raise work orders
permissions.description: Create a work order or defect.

permissions.label: View field resources
permissions.description: View Field resources — staff, licences, availability, assignments.

roleAliases.$comment: Foreign role vocabularies mapped onto canonical EqRole. Build validates every target is a real role. INVARIANT: no alias targets is_platform_admin — cross-tenant power is never derived from a tenant-held role, so tenants stay isolated. EQ-internal platform ops live out-of-band (service-role / audited impersonation), not in any tenant role.

service.label: EQ Service
service.description: EQ Service (CMMS) tenant_members.role -> canonical EqRole. super_admin maps to a tenant-scoped manager (NOT is_platform_admin); supervisor is a direct match; technician is an employee; read_only is a read-mostly apprentice.

$defaultGroups: Canonical starter security groups for seeding a fresh tenant (which today starts with zero). A group is a named bundle of EXTRA PermKeys, ADDITIVE on top of a user's base role (surfaced as session.extra_perms) — NOT a role. These templates intentionally grant only CROSS-CUTTING perms (held by a few people regardless of role) and never duplicate what a role already grants. Build validates every `perms` entry is a real permission key and that keys + names are unique. EQ Shell seeds DEFAULT_GROUPS on tenant creation. Names/descriptions are plain English (what a manager reads).

defaultGroups.name: Equipment editors
defaultGroups.description: Edit the plant & equipment list and calibration details. Add people here who maintain equipment but whose role normally only lets them view it.

defaultGroups.name: Report viewers
defaultGroups.description: View GM reports without being made a manager. Add supervisors or leads here who need to read reports.
